labels.summaryTitle: Fresh look, smoother flow: what's new

points.text: Thanks for Testing: Huge thanks for your feedback on Reddit, Threads, GitHub Discussions, and by email. Please keep it coming - I read it all and it really helps shape what comes next.
UI and UX Refresh: The app layout and interaction flow were refined across desktop and mobile, with many small usability and visual improvements.
Mobile + PWA Standalone Improvements: The mobile experience was improved significantly, especially when running the app as a homescreen shortcut.
Marker Resizing Controls: Markers can now be resized with touch gestures on mobile and with mouse wheel or +/- keyboard shortcuts on desktop.
Brand Refresh: Terraink now uses a simpler, more representative logo.
New Logo: Branding was refreshed with a simpler, more representative logo.
Theme and Layout Card Updates: Card styling and readability were refined, including improved responsive behavior and interaction consistency.
Mobile Responsiveness: Safe-area handling and mobile shortcut behavior were improved for a more app-like feel.
Small Bug Fixes: Numerous UI edge cases and interaction bugs across desktop and mobile were addressed.
Desktop Marker Resizing: Select a marker and resize with mouse wheel or + / - keys.
Mobile Marker Resizing: Marker resizing now supports touch interaction for faster on-device editing.
Closer Map Detail: Minimum map distance now goes down to 50m (from 1000m) for tighter street and building-level views.
Contributor Guide Update: Contribution documentation was refreshed for clearer collaboration.
Trademark Note: A short trademark note was added in the GitHub README and a few spots on the website as Terraink started getting more attention.
Route Display: Add route visualization between two selected points.
Themes and Fonts: Add more built-in themes, new fonts, and more options in the typography section.
Custom Themes: Let users create their own themes, save them, and import them later.
Local Save: Add the ability to save the latest creation locally.
Map Detail Controls: Add finer controls for line widths plus more toggleable map layers and details.

labels.summaryTitle: You want it? You got it! What's new:

points.text: Custom Markers: You can now add as many markers as you want, upload your own marker artwork, and control marker size and color individually or in bulk.
SVG Export: Maps can now be exported as SVG for cleaner scaling and more flexible editing workflows.
300 DPI Output: PNG and PDF exports now support 300 DPI resolution for sharper print-ready results.
Layer Toggles: You now have direct control over which map layers stay visible, making it easier to simplify or refine the map.

steps.title: Mark Up the Map Your Way

points.text: Unlimited Markers: You can now place as many markers on the map as you need.
Custom Marker Uploads: Your own marker graphics can now be uploaded and used directly on the map.
Flexible Marker Styling: Marker size and color can now be adjusted per marker or applied across all markers at once.
SVG Export: You can now export as SVG for crisp scaling and easier downstream editing.
High-Resolution Printing: PNG and PDF exports now support 300 DPI output.
Layer Visibility Toggles: You can now choose which map layers are visible, giving you finer control over the final look.
Jump to Your Location: A new button can center the map on your current location after browser permission is granted.
Display Name Rendering: Display names now stay stable after manual changes and only refresh again when the location changes.
Color Picker Fix: Issues with the color picker behavior have been resolved.
Contribution Guide Refresh: The contributor guide now includes clearer rules plus a pull request template to make submissions more consistent.
Live Map Editing: You can now pan, zoom, and rotate in real time with MapLibre, so the map updates instantly as you work.
PDF Export: Posters can now be exported as PDF for cleaner and higher-quality printing.
Theme Customization: Every selected theme can now be customized with broader color control and your own color values.
PWA and Mobile Experience: Installation flow and mobile behavior are smoother, with better handling for map interaction.
Live Map Editing: The map is now fully interactive with MapLibre, so you can pan, zoom, and rotate instantly without pressing a generate button.
No More Generate Step: The old generate-map flow has been removed because updates now render live as you edit.
Safer Map Movement: A new Edit Map button helps prevent accidental map movement while adjusting other poster settings.
Better Mobile Handling: Rotation behavior on mobile is now controlled to avoid accidental twists while still keeping full map control available.
Precise Navigation Controls: You can move the map with mouse, keyboard, or touch and quickly recenter to your starting location at any time.
Smarter Location Labels: Place names now update live with zoom level, transitioning from city to country and continent context automatically.
Layer Visibility Panel: A new collapsible panel lets you toggle water, parks, and buildings, with buildings off by default until close zoom levels.
PDF Export: You can now export posters as PDF for sharper print quality and cleaner print workflows.
Map-Only Posters: Text can now be hidden so you can export a pure map design when needed.
Distance Freedom: Distance selection is no longer capped at 50,000 meters, enabling much larger compositions.
Theme Customization: After selecting a theme, you can now customize nearly every color using presets or your own values.
New Brand Identity: The app now includes a new logo for a cleaner and more recognizable visual identity.
Help Us Grow Section: A new section makes it easier to find social channels and support the project directly.
PWA Experience: Installation behavior and standalone launch support have been improved across devices for a smoother app-like experience.
Start From Your Location: With permission enabled, the app can now initialize the map from your current location.
SEO and Metadata: Search metadata has been refined to improve clarity and discoverability in search engines.
Architecture Upgrade: For the builders and nerds among you, the codebase was migrated from JavaScript to TypeScript and reorganized around feature-first hexagonal architecture.
Contributor Experience: The repository now includes clearer contributor guidance plus an agent-focused development guide.
Roadmap Preview: Next up are simplified theme controls, SVG export, expert map-detail mode, richer typography controls with online or uploaded fonts, and locally saved custom themes.
